title: A733 MMC clock math audit

findings.finding: Public U-Boot divides the reported PERIPH0 rate by 3 for A733 MMC clock calculation and uses MMC module offsets starting at 0xd00 with 0x10 stride.
findings.decision: This is a clock-rate calculation clue, not an IDMAC data-path clue.

findings.finding: The Linux A733 CCU models fixed PERIPH0/PERIPH1 400M and 300M parents and uses SUNXI_CCU_MP_MUX_GATE_POSTDIV_DUALDIV for MMC0/MMC1 at 0xd00/0xd10 with postdiv 2. MMC2/MMC3 use 800M/600M parents at 0xd20/0xd30.
findings.decision: The U-Boot hidden-divider clue is already represented by the Linux CCU parent/postdiv model.

findings.finding: The A733 diagnostic config keeps max frequency capped at 25 MHz, and PIO reads already work. The remaining failure is descriptor fetch, not card clock negotiation.
findings.decision: Do not create another clock-rate behavior patch.

conclusion: No kernel patch was made. A733 MMC clock math remains tracked as dependent on the in-flight CCU RFC, not as the next SDMMC0 IDMAC fix candidate.
